html:
CR = hbookshelf/hdesk
Setup: bookshelf, desk, image plane, and projection rays.
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
$\mathbf{g}_b$ is the projection of the desk height on the bookshelf.
However, due to the foreshortening effect induced by perspective
projection, we cannot simply rely on the ratio of image distances to
estimate the desk's true height. Instead, we will utilize the
cross-ratio invariant to make this estimation.



:::{.column-margin}
As J. J. Gibson pointed out, the ground plane is very
useful for all kinds of 3D scene measurements.
:::

In order to
use the cross-ratio invariant we need two sets of four colinear points
related by a projective geometry. @fig-office_measuring_desk_sketch
shows a sketch of the setup we have in @fig-office_measuring_desk.

![Projection of desk height onto the bookshelf. We start with the bottom and top image coordinates of points along vertical edges of the bookshelf: 
$\mathbf{t}_b = [702~~2{,}958]^\mathsf{T}$ 
$\mathbf{g}_b= [987~~618]^\mathsf{T}$ 
and the desk: 
$\mathbf{t}_d = [679~~1{,}018]^\mathsf{T}$ 
$\mathbf{g}_d= [793~~59]^\mathsf{T}$ 
We compute the points 
$\mathbf{a}= [4{,}471~~2{,}486]^\mathsf{T}$ 
and
$\mathbf{b}= [2{,}237~~2{,}765]^\mathsf{T}$ 
as described in the text.](figures/single_view_3d/office_measuring_desk.png){width="85%" #fig-office_measuring_desk}


In the image plane, we have the four image points $\mathbf{t}_b$,
$\mathbf{b}$, $\mathbf{g}_b$ and $\mathbf{v}_3$. Those four 2D image
points correspond to the 3D locations given by the top of the bookshelf,
desk height, ground and infinity (which is the location of the vertical
vanishing point in 3D).

![Sketch of the setting from @fig-office_measuring_desk. There are two sets of colinear points related by central projection. Their cross-ratios are identical.](figures/single_view_3d/office_measuring_desk_sketch.png){width="70%" #fig-office_measuring_desk_sketch}

The rest of calculations requires computing distances between points,
which only works when using heterogeneous coordinates (or homogeneous
coordinates if the last component is equal to 1 for all points). The
cross-ratio invariant between both sets of four points gives as the
equality:

$$CR(\mathbf{P}_1,\mathbf{P}_2,\mathbf{P}_3,\mathbf{P}_4) = CR(\mathbf{p}_1,\mathbf{p}_2,\mathbf{p}_3,\mathbf{p}_4)
$${#eq-cross_ratio_shelf_desk}

The left side of the equality corresponds to the distances between the
3D points and the right side to the distances of the image points
measured in pixel units. As $\mathbf{P}_1$ is in infinity (as it
corresponds to the vanishing point), the left side ratio only has two
terms, resulting in the ratio between the bookshelf and desk heights. To

elements: 3D vertical line segment representing the bookshelf (between P_2 and P_4)
3D vertical line segment representing the desk (between ground plane and desk height)
ground plane (blue quadrilateral)
image plane as a tilted rectangle in front of the observer
lines from each 3D point to its image projection (dashed rays intersecting the image plane)
vertical vanishing point v_3 (infinity in 3D, a point on the image plane)
projection of desk height onto the bookshelf (a horizontal line or arrow connecting corresponding heights)
annotated key 3D points: P_1 (infinity), P_2 (desk/ground intersection), P_3 (desk height), P_4 (bookshelf top)
image points: p_1 (vertical vanishing point), p_2, p_3, p_4 (projection of the 3D points into image plane)
image plane intersection markers for t_b, b, g_b, and p_4
arrows indicating h_bookshelf and h_desk
dimension labels

interactions.label: Desk height (h_desk)
interactions.teaches: Shows how changing the true desk height shifts the projection point along the image and alters the cross-ratio invariant.

interactions.label: Vertical vanishing pt Y
interactions.teaches: Changes the position of the vertical vanishing point, showing how the perspective projection changes the configuration of collinear points and affects their image spacing.

interactions.label: Show projection rays
interactions.teaches: Toggles the visualization of rays from 3D points to the image plane, helping make the correspondence between 3D locations and 2D image points clear.

demo_steps.title: Setup: bookshelf and desk
demo_steps.narration: Here we see a vertical bookshelf and shorter desk both standing on a ground plane. Their tops, bottoms, and the infinitely far vanishing point are projected onto the image plane, forming four key collinear points in the image for applying the cross-ratio invariant.

demo_steps.title: Vary desk height: effect on image
demo_steps.narration: Try adjusting the true desk height. Notice how the image position for the desk’s top shifts up or down along the central vertical, but the cross-ratio between the four collinear image points remains mathematically tied to the real 3D heights.

demo_steps.title: Change vanishing point: perspective shifts
demo_steps.narration: Now, move the vertical vanishing point. This simulates a change in camera tilt or horizon line, altering the foreshortening seen in the image. The image locations of all vertical features shift, but the cross-ratio—linking 2D measurements to true 3D heights—remains unchanged.

demo_steps.title: Hide projection rays: pure projective relation
demo_steps.narration: Turn off the projection rays to focus on the geometric relationship of the four collinear points in the image. The collinearity is a direct result of central projection and enables us to apply the cross-ratio invariant to recover unknown real-world heights like h_desk.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: 3/4 view slightly above and looking down toward the ground plane, seeing both the 3D scene and the tilted image plane intersecting the projection rays. Ensure all image plane projections and vanishing points are easily visible.
interactionPlan.notes: Maintain correct collinearity of the four key points in both the image and 3D scene under all slider changes. Projection lines should update reactively to all movements. Use transparency for the image plane to keep both the 3D geometry and image points clearly visible. Draw verticals from P_2/P_3/P_4 to ground as dotted lines and use color/label coding for corresponding points.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The image plane is not correctly tilted and does not match the perspective or position shown in the source.
The thick black line from gb to tb (image plane) indicating p2-p4 is missing or extremely faint.
Dashed projection lines are present, but not all have correct geometry or mapping to the source figure's structure.
Some labels (e.g., b, gb, tb) are combined with point labels and less visually distinct; 'observer' replaces the filled black dot.
Dimension arrows and directionality for h_bookshelf and h_desk are less visually prominent and not styled to match the textbook figure.
evaluation.notes: The step-through and sliders work well, but key image plane geometry, label distinctness, and edge thicknesses diverge from the source.
action_items: Tilt and position the image plane to better match the original's perspective and orientation.
Provide a prominent, thick black line segment for p2-p4 on the image plane with correct gb, b, tb label placements.
Increase visual distinctness and styling of labels (gb, b, tb), and resolve combining them with point labels.
Enhance dimension arrows and text (h_bookshelf, h_desk) to be more prominent and styled similar to the source.
Check all dashed lines to ensure every projection ray and segment agrees in geometry and styling with the source figure.

feedback.rationale: The evaluation describes implementation and styling issues—such as image plane tilt, line thickness, label visual distinctness, and geometry of dashed lines—that can be addressed in the generation phase without reworking the overall conceptual plan.
action_items: Tilt and position the image plane to better match the original's perspective and orientation.
Provide a prominent, thick black line segment for p2-p4 on the image plane with correct gb, b, tb label placements.
Increase visual distinctness and styling of labels (gb, b, tb), and resolve combining them with point labels.
Enhance dimension arrows and text (h_bookshelf, h_desk) to be more prominent and styled similar to the source.
Check all dashed lines to ensure every projection ray and segment agrees in geometry and styling with the source figure.
actionItems: Tilt and position the image plane to better match the original's perspective and orientation.

Enhance dimension arrows and text (h_bookshelf, h_desk) to be more prominent and styled similar to the source.
Check all dashed lines to ensure every projection ray and segment agrees in geometry and styling with the source figure.

attempts.html:
Setup: bookshelf, desk, image plane, and projection rays.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The 'h_desk = ?' label is rendered with a numerical value in the interactive, not as a question mark as in the source figure.
The source figure uses thick black lines for the vertical bookshelf segment and vertical image plane segment; the generated figure's verticals are not as visually distinct.
The observer's point is not labeled in the source but is in the interactive, and the 'observer' label interferes visually.
The 'projection of desk height onto bookshelf' green label and arrow are not present in the source diagram.
The label layout and collision between text (especially h_bookshelf and h_desk) are sometimes cluttered.
evaluation.notes: Faithful interactive with excellent controls and animation, but some labeling layout and stylistic mismatches compared to the 2D figure.
action_items: Render the 'h_desk = ?' label as '?', not with a specific value, when in the initial state to match the source.
Increase the thickness of the main P2–P4 and p2–p4 vertical black lines to better match the visual emphasis of the source.
Adjust or remove superfluous interactive-only labels (e.g., 'observer', 'projection of desk height onto bookshelf') for closer faithfulness.
Improve label placement to reduce text overlap and clutter, especially around 'h_bookshelf' and 'h_desk'.
Fine-tune sphere marker and arrow head sizes for closer stylistic adherence to the reference.

feedback.rationale: All issues cited are about label rendering, visual style, and minor layout discrepancies; the concept and interactivity are correct, so only generation refinements are needed.
action_items: Render the 'h_desk = ?' label as '?', not with a specific value, when in the initial state to match the source.
Increase the thickness of the main P2–P4 and p2–p4 vertical black lines to better match the visual emphasis of the source.
Adjust or remove superfluous interactive-only labels (e.g., 'observer', 'projection of desk height onto bookshelf') for closer faithfulness.
Improve label placement to reduce text overlap and clutter, especially around 'h_bookshelf' and 'h_desk'.
Fine-tune sphere marker and arrow head sizes for closer stylistic adherence to the reference.
actionItems: Render the 'h_desk = ?' label as '?', not with a specific value, when in the initial state to match the source.
Increase the thickness of the main P2–P4 and p2–p4 vertical black lines to better match the visual emphasis of the source.
Adjust or remove superfluous interactive-only labels (e.g., 'observer', 'projection of desk height onto bookshelf') for closer faithfulness.
Improve label placement to reduce text overlap and clutter, especially around 'h_bookshelf' and 'h_desk'.
Fine-tune sphere marker and arrow head sizes for closer stylistic adherence to the reference.

attempts.html:
CR = hbookshelf/hdesk
Setup: bookshelf, desk, image plane, and projection rays.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Rendered image has extra interactive controls and side UI not in the source figure
The observer point is visible in the 3D rendering but not labeled or depicted as a black dot as distinctly as the source
Rendering adds an explicit desk vertical (green) which is less visually prominent than the thick black bookshelf vertical in the source
Arrow/line thicknesses and black/colored outline prominence is less clear/consistent than in the source figure
Font, annotation color, and bolding do not completely match (some labels/letters are smaller, offsets differ slightly)
evaluation.notes: Strong 3D, nearly all structure, labels, and projective geometry are present with rich, meaningful interactivity; minor label style/proportion mismatches.
action_items: Increase thickness and boldness of the black vertical (bookshelf) to better match the source's visual prominence
Make the observer point and its label more visually distinct, as in the source (large black dot near vanishing point v3)
Refine font styles, sizes, and label color/offsets to align even more closely with the original (especially main projective points and plane annotations)
Slightly adjust the verticals positioning so P2–P4 and their projected points more precisely match the source figure's dimensions and layout
Minimize UI intrusion on screenshot for closer visual parity (hide or float UI for figure-only export if possible)
